item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A paragraph was prefaced with a ">" character, which, due to some Wikidot glitch, rendered it invisible. I fixed that.

UPD: There's a passage saying:

printed on an upright transistor

A capacitor would seem more appropriate. The only large-scale transistors on a motherboard that I know are MOSFETs and they are usually lying flat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks, must have missed it during the clean-up stage.

Feexed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nitpick:

3 (three)

I wouldn't normally comment if this was consistent through the article (other than be dissatisfied by it), but this is the only time it happens in the whole article. Is this by accident?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has been a great month for SCPs. Love this one. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pretty cool Hexa

+70

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed this. I've seen this idea done before, but I still found this original enough to be entertaining. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted now, got too excited with congratulations when I first read it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Something has been bothering me about this, and I think I know what it is: Why would they be working with this outside of clean room conditions?

I think this is implausible enough to switch me back to no-vote, sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, Qojoc. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Supreme Overlord of Geometry, Destroyer of mathletes and standardized testing agrees.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this article a lot. It's an interesting concept that's well executed, and has a good blend of weird and silly.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think discussion of St. Cpt. Starfish is way off topic for this thread.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks, Professor Ritchie! I appreciate it.

Maybe consider leaving an upvote if you liked it?

/me uses reverse reverse psychology

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I remember seeing this one in the forums a while ago, glad to see its completed and a good read to boot! Great idea and I hope you do more!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Spoon-inflicted stigmata? That sounds painful, and tough to accomplish.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was a long night at Denny's…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"The Lucid Pancakes" sounds like something my militia in Dwarf Fortress would call themselves.

Thank you for the clarification, though. Cheers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks, Ty! I appreciate the help.

/me kicks himself for not mentioning TyGently in the special thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, Fish! Appreciate it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks bru! Here, have a brohug.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the heads up, Abettik. I'll see what I can do to clear that up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll change up the surname, thanks pudding.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry Deci that text was intentionally hidden. I was not sure which way I wanted to go with the communication. Il remove that text when I get to work. I have reverted the article to before your quote edit in the mean time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry guys, that must have been confusing. I actually purposefully hid the text about the cognitohazard because I was not sure which idea I would roll with. I fixed it to the way I wanted it though. Sorry to the cognitohazard lovers but it was mentioned in chat that it seemed more like a cop-out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Unless I'm missing something - how did the computer people answer the messages if they all died while reading it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is it Don or Donald? Both names are referred to in the article.

Upvote for adorable, tiny computer people. I particularly like the bit about the cognitohazard; it's nice seeing us have an anomalous effect on others for once.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a super good SCP and I got a lot of fun out of it. I was already going to upvote it because of "a race of silicone based, humanoid individuals measuring 400-500µm in height" but the escaped, flying wireless modem seals the deal. Each of the three folded sections made me like it more, I was gratified to see that you'd thought the idea through and explored some of the various consequences to good effect. Honestly, I think this one deserves a little more attention than it's getting. Nothing not to like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You had some text hidden in erreneous quotes, because you didn't put a space after them or accidentally put a quote marker before the text. I fixed that for you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not really, it's a holy name (the Pope's last name isn't Francis, by the way):
Sir Giuseppe Pudding, Knight of The Order of Captain Starfish of The Spoon Inflicted Stigmata, Syrup-pourer for The Lucid Pancakes, Keeper of the Egg of Yog

Generally in official documents, I don't use my holy name, though. "Researcher Jenkins" would be fine in this case.

Yes obviously there are titles that are always styled as Title Given Name. "Researcher" isn't one of them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's better than when I reviewed it, and I think I liked it then. Good work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I take it "pudding" is your surname then?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Researcher Don

I personally think it is a mistake to give characters surnames that are more commonly given names, if that character is only ever going to be referred to as [Title Surname]. In this case it is even worse, because "Don" is a nickname. It reads like [Title Nickname], like a character from a children's television show.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

I love this cute little civilization, getting scared of lice and overbreeding during turmoil, causing a little mini explosion, worshiping the grain of rice, and then flying the WiFi dongle away. :p

+ Bad Stuff
- Hide

Anyways, I was a bit confused at the beginning description, and throughout some of the descriptions of events. I think it could be cleaned up and smoothed a bit.

The explosion obliterates SCP-2978-24 and damages 3 additional communities, causing ████ SCPX-2978-B deaths.

What's SCPX? Is that a typo?

All communities are named "Motherburg", with the suffix "V1.x" added to each new iteration. On all SCP-2978-A instances, the following will be printed on an upright transistor, located near the edge of the motherboard: "Welcome to Motherburg, Population:(variable)". As of 04/10/20██, "Motherburg V1.██" is the last community to have been built.

So wait, is there one civilization across multiple motherboards, or multiple motherboards with multiple civilizations??

There's also a lot of SCP-2978-24, SCP-2978-B, SCP-2978-dsfhjk repetitions which sometimes make it difficult to read. Consider consolidating them into things like, "the affected region," or "the population," or "the motherboard."

Finally, I think the SCP foundation could contain a flying wireless router moving at 18 km/h.

Great job! I like this one a lot!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yay, it is real!

Excellent work dude. Looks awesome.

Of course, have your upboat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I dun fixed it for them

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is like a much better version of the stick figure SCP I tried. I love it +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm just going to point out something-

SCP-XXX-B, you still haven't changed it in one of the paragraph..

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey, look! My first actual SCP! No Puns, I promise.

Special thanks to these pepl: AlephA, minmin, Bypass, TwistedGears, psuul, Samsterious, Dr Hysteria, Eekium, Myriad.. Uhhh, shit I think I'm missing a few people but I still love you guys. Shouts out to #site19! Thanks for all the super helpful crit and ball-busting.

I LIED
– hide block

I read this to some of my family but it made my mother board… I'm sorry, I couldn't resist(or)!

Let's leave this thing here and see what happens.

Edit: Almost forgot Randomini! Thanks to him for the helps and also giving me the confidence to post.
